typecheck.command: echo "No typecheck configured (pure Python, no mypy)"

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the 70/30 keyword-to-engagement weight split convention. Both jade and streetwear packs allocate 70% of total weight to keyword buckets and 30% to engagement buckets. This means posts with engagement=None will score ~28-29% lower than their keyword-only maximum, which affects confidence and classification thresholds. Future workers adjusting pack weights or thresholds should be aware of this ratio.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: jade_pack.py and streetwear_pack.py both use identical 70% keyword / 30% engagement weight allocation. AGENTS.md mentions the bucket names but not the weight ratio or its impact on posts without engagement data. Review of pipeline-scoring-upgrade identified this as a non-blocking design concern: keyword-perfect posts without engagement now max at 0.58 (jade, was 0.80) and 0.495 (streetwear, was 0.70).
